# terrible_fortinet_fortios_fortios_log_fact (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `access_token` (String) Token-based authentication. Generated from GUI of Fortigate.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `enable_log` (Boolean) Enable/Disable logging for task.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `filters` (String) A list of expressions to filter the returned results. The items of the list are combined as LOGICAL AND with operator ampersand. One item itself could be concatenated with a comma as LOGICAL OR.
- `formatters` (String) A list of fields to display for returned results.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `params` (String) the parameter for each selector, see definition in above list.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `selector` (String) selector of the retrieved log type
- `selectors` (String) A list of selectors for retrieving the log type.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `sorters` (String) A list of expressions to sort the returned results. The items of the list are in ascending order with operator ampersand. One item itself could be in decending order with a comma inside.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `vdom` (String) Virtual domain, among those defined previously. A vdom is a virtual instance of the FortiGate that can be configured and used as a different unit.

### Read-Only

- `build` (String) Build number of the fortigate image
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `rows` (Number) Number of rows to return
- `serial` (String) Serial number of the unit
- `session_id` (Number) session id for the request
- `start` (Number) Row number for the first row to return
- `status` (String) Indication of the operation's result
- `subcategory` (String) Type of log that can be retrieved
- `total_lines` (Number) Total lines returned from the result
- `version` (String) Version of the FortiGate
